methodology.summary: Capability signals separate scale, density, momentum, directional readiness, and evidence coverage. Non-normalized layers are shown as explicit missing layers, not placeholders.
caveats: Capability signals are directional public-data indices, not procurement advice or claims of classified capability.
Company-level targeting and personal contact fields are intentionally excluded.
Labour, export, and regional procurement layers are source-identified but not yet normalized into region-level values.

missions.name: Arctic ISR Drones
missions.description: Uncrewed aerial, space, sensor, communications, and autonomy capacity relevant to Arctic intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance.

nationalSignals.label: Northern overlay
nationalSignals.value: Broadband and remote energy datasets catalogued, not normalized

missions.name: Secure Communications
missions.description: Secure cloud, high-assurance communications, command-and-control, cybersecurity, and communications equipment capacity.

nationalSignals.label: Sovereign capability category
nationalSignals.value: Digital Systems: Secure Cloud, AI, Quantum, C4I, high-assurance communications equipment

missions.name: Naval Autonomy
missions.description: Autonomous surface and underwater systems, naval sensors, ship systems, engineering, and simulation capacity.

nationalSignals.label: Sovereign capability category
nationalSignals.value: Specialized Manufacturing: Land Vehicles / Surface Ships, including Icebreakers and Marine Systems

nationalSignals.label: ITB relevance
nationalSignals.value: Public ITB policy motivates defence-sector investments and Canadian supplier development

scale.methodology: Sum selected mission NAICS categories from StatCan table 33-10-1095-01 for 2025-07, then normalize against the largest regional count for this capability.
scale.caveat: Large provinces often score higher by scale. Use density to find smaller regions with unusual concentration.

density.unit: relevant business locations per 10,000 regional business locations
density.methodology: Divide the relevant business-location count by the all-industry regional business-location base from the same StatCan table, then scale per 10,000.
density.caveat: Density highlights concentration, not total capacity. Small regions can rank highly with a small absolute base.

momentum.methodology: Momentum will be computed from reviewed contract, award, press-release, and source-update time series once those feeds are normalized.
momentum.caveat: No momentum number is displayed until reviewed time-series rows exist. The Atlas does not use placeholder growth signals.

readiness.methodology: Directional composite = scale signal x 0.62, Canada-wide R&D context x 0.18, and source coverage x 0.2.
readiness.caveat: This is a public evidence index, not a readiness certification, procurement recommendation, or classified capability assessment.

evidenceCoverage.unit: percent of applicable source layers with usable evidence
evidenceCoverage.methodology: 3 of 6 applicable source layers are measured or provide Canada-wide public context for this capability-region pair.
evidenceCoverage.caveat: Coverage measures what the Atlas can currently support with source-backed data. It does not prove that missing activity is absent.
